claim: The ring complexes of chromosomes in C. elegans oocytes are built in discrete layers.

result.claim: The ring complexes of chromosomes in C. elegans oocytes are built in discrete layers.
result.reasoning: My searches for propositions and chunks similar to "The ring complexes of chromosomes in C. elegans oocytes are built in discrete layers," "C. elegans oocyte chromosome ring complexes structure," and "C. elegans oocyte chromosome structure" did not yield any direct evidence supporting or refuting the claim. The retrieved information primarily focused on topics such as RNAi, epigenetic inheritance, polyploidy in megakaryocytes, and general chromosome maintenance, none of which specifically address the layered structure of ring complexes in C. elegans oocyte chromosomes. Therefore, based on the available knowledge base, there is insufficient evidence to verify the claim.

evidence.text: Researchers expressed tagged proteins in Caenorhabditis elegans.
evidence.source: Protein localization in electron micrographs using fluorescence nanoscopy

evidence.text: In the soma of adult C. elegans, all cells are post-mitotic.
evidence.source: Mitochondrial ROS and the Effectors of the Intrinsic Apoptotic Pathway in Aging Cells: The Discerning Killers!

evidence.text: C. elegans use the RNAi inheritance machinery to transmit epigenetic information.
evidence.source: A nuclear Argonaute promotes multi-generational epigenetic inheritance and germline immortality

evidence.text: Lpd is a homologue of a key regulator of neuronal migration and polarization in Caenorhabditis elegans.
evidence.source: Lpd depletion reveals that SRF specifies radial versus tangential migration of pyramidal neurons

evidence.text: The BLM complex contributes to telomere maintenance.
evidence.source: The BLM helicase contributes to telomere maintenance through processing of late-replicating intermediate structures

evidence.text: In the soma of adult C. elegans, all cells are post-mitotic.
evidence.source: Mitochondrial ROS and the Effectors of the Intrinsic Apoptotic Pathway in Aging Cells: The Discerning Killers!

evidence.text: Megakaryocytes become polyploid through repeated cycles of DNA synthesis without concomitant cell division.
evidence.source: Thrombopoietin-induced Polyploidization of Bone Marrow Megakaryocytes Is Due to a Unique Regulatory Mechanism in Late Mitosis

evidence.text: Microtubule motor proteins might be involved in polyploidization of megakaryocytes.
evidence.source: Thrombopoietin-induced Polyploidization of Bone Marrow Megakaryocytes Is Due to a Unique Regulatory Mechanism in Late Mitosis

evidence.text: The intrinsic apoptotic signalling pathway is the conduit by which at least one class of mtROS exercise their actions in the nematode Caenorhabditis elegans.
evidence.source: Mitochondrial ROS and the Effectors of the Intrinsic Apoptotic Pathway in Aging Cells: The Discerning Killers!

evidence.text: Megakaryocyte (MK) is a naturally polyploid cell that gives rise to platelets.
evidence.source: From bloodjournal.hematologylibrary.org at PENN STATE UNIVERSITY on February 23, 2013. For personal use only.

evidence.text: Pseudoknots are RNA structures composed of two helical segments connected by single-stranded regions or loops.
evidence.source: Pseudoknots: RNA Structures with Diverse Functions

evidence.text: A general feature of the CK666 phenotype in sea urchin coelomocytes was transverse actin arcs.
evidence.source: Arp2/3 complex inhibition radically alters lamellipodial actin architecture, suspended cell shape, and the cell spreading process

evidence.text: Abp1, Cbh1, and Cbh2 bind centromeres and have essential function for chromosome segregation.
evidence.source: Convergent domestication of pogo-like transposases into centromere-binding proteins in fission yeast and mammals.

evidence.text: The centromere is a chromatin region that serves as the spindle attachment point.
evidence.source: CENP-B Controls Centromere Formation Depending on the Chromatin Context

evidence.text: The reassembling nuclear envelope may enclose all the sister chromatids in a single nucleus at anaphase.
evidence.source: Thrombopoietin-induced Polyploidization of Bone Marrow Megakaryocytes Is Due to a Unique Regulatory Mechanism in Late Mitosis

evidence.text: mtDNA is organized in DNA-protein structures called nucleoids.
evidence.source: Mitochondrial DNA Replication in Health and Disease

evidence.text: All cells in the soma of adult C. elegans cannot simply be killed and replaced when showing signs of dysfunction.
evidence.source: Mitochondrial ROS and the Effectors of the Intrinsic Apoptotic Pathway in Aging Cells: The Discerning Killers!

evidence.text: Megakaryocytes are cells that undergo polyploidization.
evidence.source: Thrombopoietin-induced Polyploidization of Bone Marrow Megakaryocytes Is Due to a Unique Regulatory Mechanism in Late Mitosis

evidence.text: Double-stranded RNA-mediated gene silencing occurs in Caenorhabditis elegans.
evidence.source: A nuclear Argonaute promotes multi-generational epigenetic inheritance and germline immortality

evidence.text: CLASP1 and CLASP2 associate with the kinetochores of mitotic chromosomes.
evidence.source: Mammalian CLASPs are required for mitotic spindle organization and kinetochore alignment
